title: linum austriacum subsp. gaetulum
intro: Linum austriacum subsp. gaetulum, also known as the Austrian flax, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Linum and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as wound healing, soothing skin irritations, and reducing inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Linum austriacum subsp. gaetulum is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
